text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing data. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL, store data in structured tables with rows and columns. You use queries written in SQL to retrieve or update information. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible, storing data as documents or key-value pairs. They work well for unstructured data. Data modeling is the process of designing how your data is structured, whether in relational tables or document collections. To speed up searches, databases use indexes, which act like a book's index. A good index makes queries faster, especially with large datasets. Choosing between SQL and NoSQL depends on your project's needs.
